sutta: monks acting in 5 ways a recluse or among ought not to be taken to walk behind what 5 he keeps too far behind or too near he takes not the arms laden bowl restrains not his speech which borders on being an offense interrupts 1 time and again in speaking and is dull-witted stupid and idiot monks acting in these 5 ways a recluse not ought not to be taken to walk behind that's the end of the sutra
commentary: this suta is more of like a vinaya concerns a disciplinary rule of monks because the recruits ought not to be taken to walk behind that means he should not be taken as a disciple when a monk follows a senior monk he becomes a sort of a disciple so here a lot of this refers to body language the first 1 he keeps too far behind or too near that means when they go on arms round the junior manga he either walks too far behind or he walks too near and and steps on your on your heels or something so from this you can see here that that junior monk is not respectful if he walks too far or too near instead of keeping a proper distance because this is 1 of the ways a senior manga can tell whether the junior manga is respectful to him or not the second 1 he takes not the arms laden bowl uh this is nowadays uh it's not much seen except like in the forest tradition in thailand where the monks go on arms around and then out of respect for the senior manga usually a junior manga will volunteer to take the senior monks bowl that means when he comes back on armstrong he's already got his bowl full and then he takes the senior monk's bowl again puts it now on his on his neck under the strap on his neck so he carries 2 bowl and this is if if you go to thailand you can see this junior monks taking the senior monks bowl and on top of that now they hurry back to the monastery they arrive in the monastery before the senior monk and get the water ready for washing the feet so that when the senior monks come back they wash their feet watch the feet of the senior monks is all part of the training because uh especially when a person is new in mangudna we have a lot of our own bad habits like we are all fixed ways of thinking and and when we train as a monk we're trying to cut our egos and so it's all very good training for a junior monk to wash the feet of a senior monk let's see when he comes back as muddy and all that and and then after that when the monk steps into the hall the junior mouse will take the towel and all that and wipe his feet clean if you're not used to it you think it's a bit humiliating to do all this thing but it's all very good training and then the third 1 his restraints not his speech which borders on being an offense that means this guy he doesn't watch his feet his speech stops things which is almost uh breaks the precepts and the fourth 1 he interrupts the senior manga again and again when the senior monk is speaking he says something to interrupt the senior monk and this is not being respectful and the last 1 and this this junior monk is a fool so these if if the monk or this junior monk is such a then the senior man can ask him to leave he's not showing enough respect now if you go to a good monastery yeah in a good like thai forest monastery you find there's a lot of discipline and a lot of ways of behavior which lay person generally is not used to i think some getting used to but it's all very good training that's why you see um senior forest monks when they go anywhere there is a junior monk who accompanies them who serves them does everything for them when they're about to sit down it puts the sitting cloth for the senior monk to sit when the sydney man wants to go on arms around he brings the rope for the senior monk to wear and then brings the ball etc nexuta is
